title: Thai Green Curry

overview.description: A fragrant Thai curry with homemade green curry paste, tender chicken, coconut milk and fresh herbs. Bright, aromatic and gently spicy.

method: Heat the oil over a medium heat.
Add the Thai Green Curry Paste.
Pour in half the coconut milk and simmer until the oil begins to separate.
Add the chicken and coat well in the curry paste.
Pour in the remaining coconut milk and chicken stock.
Add the courgette, bamboo shoots and makrut lime leaves.
Simmer gently for 12–15 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the courgette is just tender.
Stir in the fish sauce and palm sugar.
Remove the makrut lime leaves if preferred.
Fold through the Thai basil immediately before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Green curry is generally hotter than red curry because it uses fresh green chillies rather than dried red ones.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to cook with homemade curry paste, coconut milk and fresh Thai herbs to create a balanced Southeast Asian curry.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick homemade curry that showcases the flavour of the frozen homemade green curry paste.
chapman-tips: One frozen portion of Thai Green Curry Paste is all you need for a family meal.
Thai basil should be stirred in at the very end to preserve its aroma.
Don't overcook the courgette—it should still have a little bite.
Makrut lime leaves perfume the curry but aren't usually eaten.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze once cooled. Fresh Thai basil is best added after reheating if available.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently over a low heat to preserve the coconut milk.
